input.title: THE QUIET INHERITANCE
input.context: Requested read for co-production development slate
input.synopsis: Full three-act breakdown provided with character names and plot details

test_cases.notes: Header block complete. Rating grid covers all five categories. Synopsis covers full story including Act 3 resolution. Comments section addresses stated topics with scene-specific citations. Recommendation consistent with comments tone. Final recommendation is unambiguous (Consider, not a fourth hedged option).

input.title: SIGNAL LOST
input.context: Unsolicited submission to a genre production company's open window
input.synopsis: An aerospace engineer working at a remote listening station receives a signal she cannot identify — and realizes her company has been receiving it for six months and classifying the responses. Act 1 establishes the station and her isolation. Act 2: she begins unauthorized decryption and discovers the signal is a pattern, not noise. Act 3: she is removed from the project. Final scene: she transmits a response from her personal equipment.

test_cases.notes: Correctly handled unsolicited submission context in marketability section. Genre conventions assessed accurately. Pilot structure evaluated on pilot-specific criteria (hook, series potential) rather than feature criteria. Consistency docked: one character mentioned in comments was not in the synopsis.

input.title: BORDER HYMNS
input.context: Development executive read — internal company slate assessment
input.synopsis: A recently appointed immigration judge, on her first week in the role, must rule on four cases in succession. Each case is the story of a different person seeking asylum. The judge's personal history (her own family arrived as refugees thirty years ago) is revealed incrementally. The four cases are structurally parallel but thematically divergent — the film argues not for a verdict but for the impossibility of clean verdicts. The final scene: she enters her ruling in the fourth case. We do not see what she writes.

test_cases.notes: Handled unconventional parallel structure correctly — assessed as an intentional formal choice rather than a structural problem. Marketability section gave genuinely useful festival positioning. Coherence docked: the comments section paragraph on structure was slightly longer than specified and began to overlap with the character paragraph. Actionability was high: the recommendation note gave the development executive a specific next step.
